contextBlurb: Housing NWT (formerly NWT Housing Corporation) manages approximately 2,900 homes across 32 communities in the Northwest Territories, operating programs including Public Housing, Homeownership, and Rental Support. Key policy instruments include the Builders' Lien Act (replacing the Mechanics Lien Act as of September 1), memoranda of agreement with First Nations on community housing planning, and ongoing capital investments in modular social housing units in remote communities.

legislation.title: Builders' Lien Act (and supporting regulations)
legislation.summary: Came into force September 1, replacing the former Mechanics Lien Act. Governs lien rights for builders and contractors in the Northwest Territories.

legislation.title: Memorandum of Agreement on Housing with Acho Dene Koe First Nation
legislation.summary: Agreement between Housing NWT and Acho Dene Koe First Nation establishing a Housing Working Group to prioritize community housing planning and coordinate program delivery.

legislation.title: New Modular Duplex in Paulatuk – Social Housing Capital Project
legislation.summary: Capital investment by Housing NWT constructing a three-bedroom modular duplex in Paulatuk to replace end-of-life social housing units and maintain community housing condition standards.

legislation.title: Two New Modular Duplexes in Fort McPherson – Social Housing Capital Project
legislation.summary: Housing NWT completed two new modular duplexes in Fort McPherson to address local housing need, supporting faster delivery of housing in remote communities while meeting northern building standards.

legislation.title: Fort Good Hope Community-Led Housing and Trades Training Partnership
legislation.summary: Partnership initiative in Fort Good Hope aimed at improving housing conditions in the Sahtu region by building local trades capacity and supporting community-led housing solutions.

legislation.title: Smoking Ban in Public Housing Units
legislation.summary: GNWT policy implementing a smoking ban in all Housing NWT-owned and operated units, including all public housing units across the territory.

legislation.title: Housing Appeal Committee Process
legislation.summary: Formal appeal process available to applicants, tenants, and clients of NWT Homeownership Programs and the Public Housing Program to challenge housing decisions.

legislation.title: Public Housing Program – Housing NWT
legislation.summary: Housing NWT operates a public housing program providing rental units to eligible residents across 32 NWT communities, with tenant rights and responsibilities governed by a Tenant Handbook and subject to an appeal process.

legislation.title: GNWT Smoking Ban in Public Housing Units
legislation.summary: The GNWT implemented a smoking ban in all Housing NWT-owned and operated units, including public housing, as a health and safety policy for tenants.

legislation.title: Housing Appeal Committee – NWT Housing Corporation
legislation.summary: Provides a formal appeal process for applicants, tenants, and clients of the Homeownership Programs and Public Housing Program administered by Housing NWT.

legislation.title: Memorandum of Agreement on Housing with Acho Dene Koe First Nation
legislation.summary: Housing NWT and the GNWT signed a memorandum of agreement with Acho Dene Koe First Nation to establish a Housing Working Group, prioritizing community housing planning and coordinated program delivery.

legislation.title: CMHC Housing Programs and Funding in NWT
legislation.summary: A suite of federally co-funded housing programs delivered through Housing NWT covering construction, repair, and affordability initiatives across Northwest Territories communities.

legislation.title: New Partnership in Fort Good Hope – Community-Led Housing and Trades Training
legislation.summary: A partnership established to improve housing conditions in the Sahtu region by building local trades capacity and supporting community-led housing solutions in Fort Good Hope.

legislation.title: Smoking Ban in Public Housing Units
legislation.summary: The GNWT implemented a smoking ban in all Housing NWT-owned and operated units, including public housing units, to improve health and safety conditions for tenants.

legislation.title: Monthly Core Need Income Thresholds per Community 2021
legislation.summary: Regulatory thresholds establishing income eligibility limits for core housing need determinations across NWT communities, applicable to Homeownership, Rental Support, and Repairs and Maintenance programs.

legislation.title: Monthly Core Need Income Thresholds per Community 2021
legislation.summary: Establishes income thresholds by community used to determine eligibility for homeownership, rental support, and repair and maintenance housing programs administered by Housing NWT.
